name: Chocolate Marble Praline Cheesecake
recipeIngredient: 1 package DH Golden Sugar Cookie Mix
recipeInstructions:
For added flavor, toast pecans before chopping.
Spread pecans in a single layer on a baking sheet. Toast them at 350°F for 3-5 minutes or until fragrant. Cool completely.
1. Preheat the oven to 350°F.
2. For Crust: Combine cookie mix, contents of buttery flavor packet from Mix, 1 egg, and chopped pecans in a large bowl. Stir until thoroughly blended. Press mixture into the bottom of an ungreased 9-inch springform pan. Bake at 350°F for 20-22 minutes or until the edge is light brown and the center is set. Remove from oven.
3. For Filling: Combine brown sugar and flour in a small bowl; set aside. Place cream cheese in a large bowl.
Beat at low speed with an electric mixer, adding the brown sugar mixture gradually. Remove one cup of batter to a small bowl, add melted chocolate. Pour the remaining plain batter onto the warm crust. Drop spoonfuls of chocolate batter over the plain batter. Run a knife through the batters to marble. Arrange pecan halves around the top edge.
Bake at 350°F for 45-55 minutes or until set. Loosen the cake from the sides of the pan with a knife or spatula. Cool completely on a rack. Refrigerate 2 hours or until ready to serve.
4. To serve, remove the sides of the pan. Glaze the top of the cheesecake with store-bought caramel flavor topping. Cut into slices and serve with additional caramel flavor topping, if desired.
From Duncan Hines - Favorite Brand Name Recipes - Fabulous Fall Baking.
